```json
{
  "call_id": "call_ib0hxsrDzjtmlk5DVO4AZO4z",

  ],

  "parsed_cmd": [
    {
      "type": "read",
      "cmd": "sed -n '1,260p' docs/ai/skills/performance-review/SKILL.md",
      "name": "SKILL.md",
      "path": "docs/ai/skills/performance-review/SKILL.md"
    }
  ],
  "source": "unified_exec_startup",
  "stdout": "---\nname: performance-review\ndescription: Use when investigating concrete or user-visible runtime performance issues, or reviewing changes for likely performance regressions in request, refresh, polling, or other hot paths in this repo, including slow responses, high CPU or memory, allocation pressure, throughput limits, remote call fan-out, and repeated work.\n---\n\n# Performance Review\n\nUse this skill when the task is about concrete or user-visible runtime performance problems in this repository.\n\nTypical triggers:\n- a page, route, refresh cycle, or polling path feels slow, heavy, or sluggish\n- CPU, memory, allocations, or throughput look worse than expected\n- a recent change may have introduced a performance regression\n- the user asks for profiling, benchmarking, or regression analysis on a known or suspected hot path\n- the app appears to be doing too much repeated work or too many remote calls\n\nDo not use this skill for:\n- generic code review with no performance concern\n- vague requests like \"make this faster\" or \"make this more efficient\" without a symptom or target path\n- speculative micro-optimization without evidence\n- architecture brainstorming or caching/concurrency design before a bottleneck has been identified\n\n## Goal\n\nFind the actual bottleneck, support the conclusion with measurements where possible, and propose the smallest focused fixes that are likely to improve the result without unnecessary complexity.\n\n## Operating Rules\n\n- Treat this as an evidence-driven skill, not a generic optimization pass.\n- Do not optimize vague requests like \"make this faster\" or \"make this more efficient\" without a stated symptom, hot path, or regression concern.\n- If the request is too vague, ask for the slow path, user-visible symptom, suspected regression, or performance goal before proceeding.\n- Diagnose the bottleneck first and propose focused fixes before making code changes.\n- Do not implement performance changes unless the user explicitly asks for them after reviewing the findings.\n- Do not introduce caching, concurrency, or broad refactors unless the evidence justifies the added complexity.\n- Keep behavior unchanged unless the user explicitly accepts a tradeoff.\n- If direct measurement is not possible, state that clearly and separate observation, inference, and recommendation.\n- When proposing fixes, include expected benefit, tradeoffs, and how the change should be verified.\n\n## Workflow\n\n1. Establish the symptom, complaint, or regression concern.\n2. Identify the affected request path, refresh loop, polling path, or operation.\n3. Check whether there is enough signal to proceed.\n4. If the request is too vague, ask for the slow path, user-visible symptom, suspected regression, or performance goal before analyzing further.\n5. Measure or inspect the hot path before suggesting fixes.\n6. Separate measured facts from inference and identify the most likely bottleneck.\n7. Propose the smallest focused fixes, with expected benefit, tradeoffs, and clear verification steps.\n8. Only implement changes if the user explicitly asks after reviewing the findings.\n9. If changes are made, re-measure and report before/after results.\n\n## Measurement First\n\nPrefer direct evidence such as:\n- request latency before and after\n- `go test -bench` results\n- `-benchmem` allocation data\n- `pprof` CPU or heap profiles\n- counts of external calls per request\n- concrete timing, allocation, or query-count comparisons\n\nWhen measurement is possible, prefer it over intuition.\n\nExternal observability is valid evidence when available.\nIf the environment exposes Prometheus, tracing, ingress or gateway metrics, load balancer metrics, or service-level latency and error data through MCPs or other tools, use those signals to confirm the user-visible symptom and narrow the hot path before proposing fixes.\n\nWhen direct measurement is not practical:\n- inspect the hot path carefully\n- identify the repeated work, remote call fan-out, allocation pressure, or contention risk\n- state clearly that the conclusion is based on inspection rather than measurement\n- separate measured facts, observed code behavior, and inference\n\nDo not assume any specific metric, dashboard, or controller exists. Use the observability available in the current environment.\n\nDo not present guesses as facts.\n\n## Repo-Specific Focus\n\nFor `homelab-dashboard`, check these first:\n- Prometheus query count, latency, duplicated fetches, and page-specific fan-out\n- Kubernetes API calls that happen in request or refresh paths instead of being reduced, filtered, or reused\n- repeated data shaping, sorting, formatting, or aggregation before template rendering\n- polling and refresh behavior that amplifies backend load or repeats the same work too often\n- page assembly work that fetches or computes the same data separately for hub, security, anomaly, or forecasting views\n- string building, JSON marshalling, and other allocation-heavy transforms in hot paths\n- shared clients, synchronization, or contention that can serialize otherwise independent work\n\n## Review Heuristics\n\nWhen reviewing code, prefer findings that materially affect user-visible performance:\n- repeated remote calls that could be batched, reduced, cached, or reused safely\n- N+1 style loops over Prometheus, Kubernetes, or page assembly data\n- work repeated on every request, refresh cycle, or polling interval that could be reduced or reused\n- allocation-heavy transforms or repeated creation of large temporary slices, maps, or buffers in hot paths\n- expensive formatting, sorting, parsing, or aggregation done more often than necessary\n- serial work that could dominate latency because independent operations are forced through one path\n\nPrefer findings with a clear path to verification, such as lower latency, fewer remote calls, fewer allocations, or less repeated work.\n\nIgnore cosmetic micro-optimizations unless they sit on a proven hot path.\n\n## Change Discipline\n\n- Do not optimize blindly or propose changes without a clear bottleneck or symptom.\n- Prefer the smallest fix that addresses the measured or strongly supported problem.\n- Keep behavior unchanged unless the user explicitly accepts a functional tradeoff.\n- Prefer simple changes over clever ones, especially in hot paths that are already hard to reason about.\n- Do not recommend caching without explaining invalidation, staleness, and why simpler reuse is not enough.\n- Do not recommend concurrency without explaining why the work is independent, what latency it improves, and what new complexity it introduces.\n- Avoid broad refactors framed as performance work unless the existing structure is itself the bottleneck.\n- If implementation is requested, verify the effect after the change rather than assuming the proposal helped.\n\n## Output Expectations\n\nA good performance review should present findings before fixes and include:\n- the observed symptom, complaint, or regression concern\n- the affected code path, request path, refresh loop, polling path, or operation\n- the measured evidence, or a clear statement that the conclusion is based on inspection rather than direct measurement\n- the most likely bottleneck, with measured facts separated from inference\n- the proposed fixes, ordered from smallest to most invasive when there is more than one reasonable option\n- the expected benefit, tradeoffs, and verification steps for each proposed fix\n- before and after evidence if changes were implemented\n- any remaining uncertainty, missing measurements, or follow-up work\n",

The container will run rootless and with an RO filesystem\n\n### Helpers\nYou should be able to access my cluster through tools, I have kubernetes-mcp and flux-operator-mcp running, along some others. Use Context7 when unsure about how things should be, you should have up to date documentation there. Feel free to use subagents as well\n",
  "status": "completed"
}
```

err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, err\n\t}\n\n\tsamples := make([]Sample, 0, len(response.Data.Result))\n\tfor _, raw := range response.Data.Result {\n\t\ttimestamp, value, err := parseValuePair(raw.Value)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"parse sample %q: %w\", query, err)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tsamples = append(samples, Sample{\n\t\t\tMetric:    raw.Metric,\n\t\t\tTimestamp: timestamp,\n\t\t\tValue:     value,\n\t\t})\n\t}\n\n\treturn samples, nil\n}\n\nfunc (c *Client) QueryRange(ctx context.Context, query string, start, end time.Time, step time.Duration) ([]RangeSeries, error) {\n\tresponse := promRangeResponse{}\n\tvalues := url.Values{\n\t\t\"query\": []string{query},\n\t\t\"start\": []string{strconv.FormatInt(start.Unix(), 10)},\n\t\t\"end\":   []string{strconv.FormatInt(end.Unix(), 10)},\n\t\t\"step\":  []string{strconv.FormatFloat(step.Seconds(), 'f', -1, 64)},\n\t}\n\n\tif err := c.do(ctx, \"/api/v1/query_range\", values, &response); err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, err\n\t}\n\n\tseries := make([]RangeSeries, 0, len(response.Data.Result))\n\tfor _, raw := range response.Data.Result {\n\t\tpoints := make([]Point, 0, len(raw.Values))\n\t\tfor _, pair := range raw.Values {\n\t\t\ttimestamp, value, err := parseValuePair(pair)\n\t\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"parse range sample %q: %w\", query, err)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tpoints = append(points, Point{Timestamp: timestamp, Value: value})\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tseries = append(series, RangeSeries{\n\t\t\tMetric: raw.Metric,\n\t\t\tPoints: points,\n\t\t})\n\t}\n\n\treturn series, nil\n}\n\nfunc (c *Client) Scalar(ctx context.Context, query string) (float64, error) {\n\tsamples, err := c.Query(ctx, query)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn 0, err\n\t}\n\n\tif len(samples) == 0 {\n\t\treturn 0, nil\n\t}\n\n\treturn samples[0].Value, nil\n}\n\nfunc (c *Client) do(ctx context.Context, path string, values url.Values, target any) error {\n\tendpoint, err := url.Parse(c.baseURL + path)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"parse endpoint: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\tendpoint.RawQuery = values.Encode()\n\n\treq, err := http.NewRequestWithContext(ctx, http.MethodGet, endpoint.String(), nil)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"build request: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\tresp, err := c.httpClient.Do(req)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"prometheus request failed: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\tdefer resp.Body.Close()\n\n\tif resp.StatusCode >= http.StatusBadRequest {\n\t\tbody, _ := io.ReadAll(io.LimitReader(resp.Body, 4096))\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"prometheus returned %s: %s\", resp.Status, strings.TrimSpace(string(body)))\n\t}\n\n\tif err := json.NewDecoder(resp.Body).Decode(target); err != nil {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"decode response: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\treturn nil\n}\n\nfunc parseValuePair(raw []any) (time.Time, float64, error) {\n\tif len(raw) != 2 {\n\t\treturn time.Time{}, 0, fmt.Errorf(\"unexpected pair length %d\", len(raw))\n\t}\n\n\ttsFloat, ok := raw[0].(float64)\n\tif !ok {\n\t\treturn time.Time{}, 0, fmt.Errorf(\"timestamp is %T\", raw[0])\n\t}\n\n\tvalueString, ok := raw[1].(string)\n\tif !ok {\n\t\treturn time.Time{}, 0, fmt.Errorf(\"value is %T\", raw[1])\n\t}\n\n\tvalue, err := strconv.ParseFloat(valueString, 64)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tif strings.EqualFold(valueString, \"nan\") {\n\t\t\tvalue = math.NaN()\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\treturn time.Time{}, 0, err\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tseconds := int64(tsFloat)\n\tnanos := int64((tsFloat - float64(seconds)) * float64(time.Second))\n\treturn time.Unix(seconds, nanos).UTC(), value, nil\n}\n\ntype promResponse struct {\n\tStatus string `json:\"status\"`\n\tData   struct {\n\t\tResultType string           `json:\"resultType\"`\n\t\tResult     []instantResult  `json:\"result\"`\n\t} `json:\"data\"`\n\tErrorType string `json:\"errorType\"`\n\tError     string `json:\"error\"`\n}\n\ntype promRangeResponse struct {\n\tStatus string `json:\"status\"`\n\tData   struct {\n\t\tResultType string          `json:\"resultType\"`\n\t\tResult     []rangeResult   `json:\"result\"`\n\t} `json:\"data\"`\n\tErrorType string `json:\"errorType\"`\n\tError     string `json:\"error\"`\n}\n\ntype instantResult struct {\n\tMetric map[string]string `json:\"metric\"`\n\tValue  []any             `json:\"value\"`\n}\n\ntype rangeResult struct {\n\tMetric map[string]string `json:\"metric\"`\n\tValues [][]any           `json:\"values\"`\n}\n",

Client struct {\n\tclientset *kubernetes.Clientset\n\tdynamic   dynamic.Interface\n}\n\ntype WarningEvent struct {\n\tNamespace string\n\tReason    string\n\tMessage   string\n\tObject    string\n\tWhen      time.Time\n}\n\ntype FluxResource struct {\n\tKind           string\n\tNamespace      string\n\tName           string\n\tReady          bool\n\tSuspended      bool\n\tStatus         string\n\tMessage        string\n\tLastTransition time.Time\n}\n\ntype VolsyncSource struct {\n\tName             string\n\tNamespace        string\n\tSchedule         string\n\tSourcePVC        string\n\tMethod           string\n\tStatus           string\n\tMessage          string\n\tLastResult       string\n\tLastSyncTime     time.Time\n\tNextSyncTime     time.Time\n\tLastSyncDuration       string\n\tStatus         string\n\tMessage        string\n\tLastTransition time.Time\n}\n\nfunc NewClient(kubeconfigPath string) (*Client, error) {\n\tcfg, err := buildConfig(kubeconfigPath)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, err\n\t}\n\n\tclientset, err := kubernetes.NewForConfig(cfg)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"build clientset: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\tdyn, err := dynamic.NewForConfig(cfg)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"build dynamic client: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\treturn &Client{\n\t\tclientset: clientset,\n\t\tdynamic:   dyn,\n\t}, nil\n}\n\nfunc (c *Client) WarningEvents(ctx context.Context, limit int) ([]WarningEvent, error) {\n\tif c == nil || c.clientset == nil {\n\t\treturn nil, errors.New(\"kubernetes client unavailable\")\n\t}\n\n\tevents, err := c.clientset.CoreV1().Events(\"\").List(ctx, metav1.ListOptions{})\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, err\n\t}\n\n\tresult := make([]WarningEvent, 0, len(events.Items))\n\tfor _, event := range events.Items {\n\t\tif event.Type != corev1.EventTypeWarning {\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\n\t\twhen := event.LastTimestamp.Time\n\t\tif when.IsZero() {\n\t\t\twhen = event.EventTime.Time\n\t\t}\n\t\tif when.IsZero() {\n\t\t\twhen = event.CreationTimestamp.Time\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tresult = append(result, WarningEvent{\n\t\t\tNamespace: event.Namespace,\n\t\t\tReason:    event.Reason,\n\t\t\tMessage:   event.Message,\n\t\t\tObject:    fmt.Sprintf(\"%s/%s\", event.InvolvedObject.Kind, event.InvolvedObject.Name),\n\t\t\tWhen:      when,\n\t\t})\n\t}\n\n\tsort.Slice(result, func(i, j int) bool {\n\t\treturn result[i].When.After(result[j].When)\n\t})\n\n\tif limit > 0 && len(result) > limit {\n\t\tresult = result[:limit]\n\t}\n\n\treturn result, nil\n}\n\nfunc (c *Client) FluxResources(ctx context.Context, limit int, allowlist []string) ([]FluxResource, error) {\n\tif c == nil || c.dynamic == nil {\n\t\treturn nil, errors.New(\"kubernetes dynamic client unavailable\")\n\t}\n\n\tgvrs := []schema.GroupVersionResource{\n\t\t{Group: \"kustomize.toolkit.fluxcd.io\", Version: map[string]*externalSecretSnapshot{}\n\tenvoyRouteSnapshots := map[string]*envoyRouteSnapshot{}\n\ttoolhiveBackendSnapshots := map[string]*toolhiveBackendSnapshot{}\n\trenovateProjectSnapshots := map[string]*renovateProjectSnapshot{}\n\tensureCNPGSnapshot := func(metric map[string]string) *cnpgClusterSnapshot {\n\t\tcluster := cnpgClusterName(metric)\n\t\tif cluster == \"\" {\n\t\t\treturn nil\n\t\t}\n\t\tnamespace := strings.TrimSpace(metric[\"namespace\"])\n\t\tkey := namespace + \"/\" + cluster\n\t\tsnapshot, ok := cnpgSnapshots[key]\n\t\tif !ok {\n\t\t\tsnapshot = &cnpgClusterSnapshot{\n\t\t\t\tName:      cluster,\n\t\t\t\tNamespace: namespace,\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tcnpgSnapshots[key] = snapshot\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn snapshot\n\t}\n\tensureVolsyncSnapshot := func(namespace, name string) *volsyncSourceSnapshot {\n\t\tnamespace = strings.TrimSpace(namespace)\n\t\tname = strings.TrimSpace(name)\n\t\tif namespace == \"\" || name == \"\" {\n\t\t\treturn nil\n\t\t}\n\t\tkey := namespace + \"/\" + name\n\t\tsnapshot, ok := volsyncSnapshots[key]\n\t\tif !ok {\n\t\t\tsnapshot = &volsyncSourceSnapshot{\n\t\t\t\tName:      name,\n\t\t\t\tNamespace: namespace,\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tvolsyncSnapshots[key] = snapshot\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn snapshot\n\t}\n\tensureExternalSecretSnapshot := func(namespace, name string) *externalSecretSnapshot {\n\t\tnamespace = strings.TrimSpace(namespace)\n\t\tname = strings.TrimSpace(name)\n\t\tif namespace == \"\" || name == \"\" {\n\t\t\treturn nil\n\t\t}\n\t\tkey := namespace + \"/\" + name\n\t\tsnapshot, ok := externalSecretSnapshots[key]\n\t\tif !ok {\n\t\t\tsnapshot = &externalSecretSnapshot{\n\t\t\t\tName:      name,\n\t\t\t\tNamespace: namespace,\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\texternalSecretSnapshots[key] = snapshot\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn snapshot\n\t}\n\tensureEnvoyRouteSnapshot := func(name string) *envoyRouteSnapshot {\n\t\tname = strings.TrimSpace(name)\n\t\tif name == \"\" || name == \"prometheus_stats\" {\n\t\t\treturn nil\n\t\t}\n\t\tsnapshot, ok := envoyRouteSnapshots[name]\n\t\tif !ok {\n\t\t\trouteNamespace, routeName := parseEnvoyRoute(name)\n\t\t\tsnapshot = &envoyRouteSnapshot{\n\t\t\t\tName:      name,\n\t\t\t\tNamespace: routeNamespace,\n\t\t\t\tRoute:     routeName,\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tenvoyRouteSnapshots[name] = snapshot\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn snapshot\n\t}\n\tensureToolhiveBackendSnapshot := func(name string) *toolhiveBackendSnapshot {\n\t\tname = strings.TrimSpace(name)\n\t\tif name == \"\" {\n\t\t\treturn nil\n\t\t}\n\t\tsnapshot, ok := toolhiveBackendSnapshots[name]\n\t\tif !ok {\n\t\t\tsnapshot = &toolhiveBackendSnapshot{Name: name}\n\t\t\ttoolhiveBackendSnapshots[name] = snapshot\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn snapshot\n\t}\n\tensureRenovateProjectSnapshot := func(name string) *renovateProjectSnapshot {\n\t\tname = strings.TrimSpace(name)\n\t\tif name == \"\" {\n\t\t\treturn nil\n\t\t}\n\t\tsnapshot, ok := renovateProjectSnapshots[name]\n\t\tif !ok {\n\t\t\tsnapshot = &renovateProjectSnapshot{Name: name}\n\t\t\trenovateProjectSnapshots[name] = snapshot\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn snapshot\n\t}\n\n\trecordScalar := func(query string, dest *float64) {\n\t\tvalue, err := s.prom.Scalar(ctx, query)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\terrors = append(errors, fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: %v\", query, err))\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\t\tif math.IsNaN(value) {\n\t\t\tvalue = 0\n\t\t}\n\t\t*dest = value\n\t}\n\n\trecordVector := func(query string, apply func([]prom.Sample)) {\n\t\tvalues, err := s.prom.Query(ctx, query)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\terrors = append(errors, fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: %v\", query, err))\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\t\tapply(values)\n\t}\n\n\trecordRange := func(query string, dest *[]float64) {\n\t\tend := time.Now().UTC()\n\t\tstart := end.Add(-24 * time.Hour)\n\t\tseries, err := s.prom.QueryRange(ctx, query, start, end, time.Hour)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\terrors = append(errors, fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: %v\", query, err))\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\t\t*dest = flattenSeries(series)\n\t}\n\n\trecordScalar(`sum(kube_node_status_condition{condition=\"Ready\",status=\"true\"})`, &data.nodesReady)\n\trecordScalar(`count(kube_node_info)`, &data.nodesTotal)\n\trecordScalar(`count(kube_namespace_status_phase{phase=\"Active\"})`, &data.namespaces)\n\trecordScalar(`sum(kube_pod_status_phase{phase=\"Running\"})`, &data.podsRunning)\n\trecordScalar(`sum(kube_pod_status_phase{phase=\"Pending\"})`, &data.podsPending)\n\trecordScalar(`sum(kube_pod_status_phase{phase=~\"Failed|Unknown\"})`, &data.podsFailed)\n\trecordScalar(`sum(up)`, &data.targetsHealthy)\n\trecordScalar(`count(up)`, &data.targetsTotal)\n\trecordScalar(`100 * (1 - avg(rate(node_cpu_seconds_total{mode=\"idle\"}[5m])))`, &data.clusterCPU)\n\trecordScalar(`100 * (1 - (sum(node_memory_MemAvailable_bytes) / sum(node_memory_MemTotal_bytes)))`, &data.clusterMemory)\n\trecordScalar(`sum(flux_resource_info{ready=\"True\"})`, &data.fluxReady)\n\trecordScalar(`sum(flux_resource_info{ready!=\"True\"})`, &data.fluxNotReady)\n\trecordScalar(`sum(flux_resource_info{suspended=\"True\"})`, &data.fluxSuspended)\n\trecordScalar(`sum(flux_resource_info)`, &data.fluxTotal)\n\trecordScalar(`sum(up{namespace=\"flux-system\"})`, &data.fluxControllersUp)\n\trecordScalar(`count(up{namespace=\"flux-system\"}) - sum(up{namespace=\"flux-system\"})`, &data.fluxControllersDown)\n\trecordScalar(`count(up == 0)`, &data.downTargetCount)\n\trecordScalar(`count(sum by(namespace,pod) (increase(kube_pod_container_status_restarts_total[30m])) > 0)`, &data.restartBurstCount)\n\trecordScalar(`sum(externalsecret_status_condition{condition=\"Ready\",status=\"True\"})`, &data.externalSecretsReady)\n\trecordScalar(`sum(externalsecret_status_condition{condition=\"Ready\",status!=\"True\"})`, &data.externalSecretsDegraded)\n\trecordScalar(`sum(increase(externalsecret_sync_calls_error[24h]))`, &data.externalSecretSyncErrors24h)\n\trecordScalar(`count(volsync_missed_intervals_total{role=\"source\"})`, &data.volsyncSources)\n\trecordScalar(`sum(volsync_volume_out_of_sync{role=\"source\"})`, &data.volsyncOutOfSync)\n\trecordScalar(`sum(increase(volsync_missed_intervals_total{role=\"source\"}[24h]))`, &data.volsyncMissed24h)\n\trecordScalar(`count(count by(job) (cnpg_pg_replication_streaming_replicas))`, &data.cnpgClusters)\n\trecordScalar(`sum(count by(job, namespace) (cnpg_collector_up))`, &data.cnpgInstances)\n\trecordScalar(`sum(max by(job) (cnpg_pg_replication_streaming_replicas))`, &data.cnpgStreamingReplicas)\n\trecordScalar(`max(cnpg_pg_stat_replication_write_lag_seconds)`, &data.cnpgMaxReplicationLag)\n\trecordScalar(`sum(rate(envoy_cluster_external_upstream_rq[5m]))`, &data.envoyRequestRate)\n\trecordScalar(`sum(rate(envoy_cluster_external_upstream_rq_xx{envoy_response_code_class=\"5\"}[5m]))`, &data.envoyErrorRate)\n\trecordScalar(`histogram_quantile(0.95, sum(rate(envoy_cluster_external_upstream_rq_time_bucket[5m])) by (le))`, &data.envoyP95Latency)\n\trecordScalar(`sum(toolhive_mcp_active_connections)`, &data.toolhiveConnections)\n\trecordScalar(`count(count by(target_workload_name) (toolhive_vmcp_backend_requests_total))`, &data.toolhiveBackends)\n\trecordScalar(`sum(increase(toolhive_vmcp_backend_errors_total[24h]))`, &data.toolhiveBackendErrors24h)\n\trecordScalar(`sum(rate(toolhive_vmcp_backend_requests_duration_seconds_sum[5m])) / sum(rate(toolhive_vmcp_backend_requests_duration_seconds_count[5m]))`, &data.toolhiveAvgBackendLatency)\n\trecordScalar(`count(renovate_operator_run_failed)`, &data.renovateProjects)\n\trecordScalar(`sum(increase(renovate_operator_project_executions_total[24h]))`, &data.renovateExecutions24h)\n\trecordScalar(`sum(renovate_operator_run_failed)`, &data.renovateRunsFailed)\n\trecordScalar(`sum(renovate_operator_dependency_issues)`, &data.renovateDependencyIssues)\n\trecordScalar(`count(sum by(envoy_cluster_name) (rate(envoy_cluster_external_upstream_rq[5m])) > 0)`, sample.Value),\n\t\t\t\tDetail: \"5m CPU saturation\",\n\t\t\t\tTone:   toneByThreshold(sample.Value, s.cfg.Thresholds.NodeCPUWarnPercent),\n\t\t\t})\n\t\t}\n\t})\n\n\trecordVector(`topk(5, ((1 - strings.EqualFold(status, \"True\") && sample.Value > 0:\n\t\t\t\tsnapshot.Ready = true\n\t\t\tcase sample.Value > 0:\n\t\t\t\tsnapshot.Ready = false\n\t\t\t\tsnapshot.Status = s.cfg.DemoMode {\n\t\treturn demoSharedData(), nil\n\t}\n\n\treturn s.collectShared(ctx)\n}\n\nfunc (s *Service) collectShared(ctx context.Context) (sharedData, []string) {\n\tvar (\n\t\tdata   sharedData\n\t\terrors []string\n\t)\n\n\tcnpgSnapshots := map[string]*cnpgClusterSnapshot{}\n\tvolsyncSnapshots := map[string]*volsyncSourceSnapshot{}\n\texternalSecretSnapshots := map[string]*externalSecretSnapshot{}\n\tenvoyRouteSnapshots := map[string]*envoyRouteSnapshot{}\n\ttoolhiveBackendSnapshots := map[string]*toolhiveBackendSnapshot{}\n\trenovateProjectSnapshots := map[string]*renovateProjectSnapshot{}\n\tensureCNPGSnapshot := func(metric map[string]string) *cnpgClusterSnapshot {\n\t\tcluster := cnpgClusterName(metric)\n\t\tif cluster == \"\" {\n\t\t\treturn nil\n\t\t}\n\t\tnamespace := strings.TrimSpace(metric[\"namespace\"])\n\t\tkey := namespace + \"/\" + cluster\n\t\tsnapshot, ok := cnpgSnapshots[key]\n\t\tif !ok {\n\t\t\tsnapshot = &cnpgClusterSnapshot{\n\t\t\t\tName:      cluster,\n\t\t\t\tNamespace: namespace,\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tcnpgSnapshots[key] = snapshot\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn snapshot\n\t}\n\tensureVolsyncSnapshot := func(namespace, name string) *volsyncSourceSnapshot {\n\t\tnamespace = strings.TrimSpace(namespace)\n\t\tname = strings.TrimSpace(name)\n\t\tif namespace == \"\" || name == \"\" {\n\t\t\treturn nil\n\t\t}\n\t\tkey := namespace + \"/\" + name\n\t\tsnapshot, ok := volsyncSnapshots[key]\n\t\tif !ok {\n\t\t\tsnapshot = &volsyncSourceSnapshot{\n\t\t\t\tName:      name,\n\t\t\t\tNamespace: namespace,\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tvolsyncSnapshots[key] = snapshot\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn snapshot\n\t}\n\tensureExternalSecretSnapshot := func(namespace, name string) *externalSecretSnapshot {\n\t\tnamespace = strings.TrimSpace(namespace)\n\t\tname = strings.TrimSpace(name)\n\t\tif namespace == \"\" || name == \"\" {\n\t\t\treturn nil\n\t\t}\n\t\tkey := namespace + \"/\" + name\n\t\tsnapshot, ok := externalSecretSnapshots[key]\n\t\tif !ok {\n\t\t\tsnapshot = &externalSecretSnapshot{\n\t\t\t\tName:      name,\n\t\t\t\tNamespace: namespace,\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\texternalSecretSnapshots[key] = snapshot\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn snapshot\n\t}\n\tensureEnvoyRouteSnapshot := func(name string) *envoyRouteSnapshot {\n\t\tname = strings.TrimSpace(name)\n\t\tif name == \"\" || name == \"prometheus_stats\" {\n\t\t\treturn nil\n\t\t}\n\t\tsnapshot, ok := envoyRouteSnapshots[name]\n\t\tif !ok {\n\t\t\trouteNamespace, routeName := parseEnvoyRoute(name)\n\t\t\tsnapshot = &envoyRouteSnapshot{\n\t\t\t\tName:      name,\n\t\t\t\tNamespace: routeNamespace,\n\t\t\t\tRoute:     routeName,\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tenvoyRouteSnapshots[name] = snapshot\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn snapshot\n\t}\n\tensureToolhiveBackendSnapshot := func(name string) *toolhiveBackendSnapshot {\n\t\tname = strings.TrimSpace(name)\n\t\tif name == \"\" {\n\t\t\treturn nil\n\t\t}\n\t\tsnapshot, ok := toolhiveBackendSnapshots[name]\n\t\tif !ok {\n\t\t\tsnapshot = &toolhiveBackendSnapshot{Name: name}\n\t\t\ttoolhiveBackendSnapshots[name] = snapshot\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn snapshot\n\t}\n\tensureRenovateProjectSnapshot := func(name string) *renovateProjectSnapshot {\n\t\tname = strings.TrimSpace(name)\n\t\tif name == \"\" {\n\t\t\treturn nil\n\t\t}\n\t\tsnapshot, ok := renovateProjectSnapshots[name]\n\t\tif !ok {\n\t\t\tsnapshot = &renovateProjectSnapshot{Name: name}\n\t\t\trenovateProjectSnapshots[name] = snapshot\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn snapshot\n\t}\n\n\trecordScalar := func(query string, dest *float64) {\n\t\tvalue, err := s.prom.Scalar(ctx, query)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\terrors = append(errors, fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: %v\", query, err))\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\t\tif math.IsNaN(value) {\n\t\t\tvalue = 0\n\t\t}\n\t\t*dest = value\n\t}\n\n\trecordVector := func(query string, apply func([]prom.Sample)) {\n\t\tvalues, err := s.prom.Query(ctx, query)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\terrors = append(errors, fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: %v\", query, err))\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\t\tapply(values)\n\t}\n\n\trecordRange := func(query string, dest *[]float64) {\n\t\tend := time.Now().UTC()\n\t\tstart := end.Add(-24 * time.Hour)\n\t\tseries, err := s.prom.QueryRange(ctx, query, start, end, time.Hour)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\terrors = append(errors, fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: %v\", query, err))\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\t\t*dest = flattenSeries(series)\n\t}\n\n\trecordScalar(`sum(kube_node_status_condition{condition=\"Ready\",status=\"true\"})`, &data.nodesReady)\n\trecordScalar(`count(kube_node_info)`, &data.nodesTotal)\n\trecordScalar(`count(kube_namespace_status_phase{phase=\"Active\"})`, &data.namespaces)\n\trecordScalar(`sum(kube_pod_status_phase{phase=\"Running\"})`, &data.podsRunning)\n\trecordScalar(`sum(kube_pod_status_phase{phase=\"Pending\"})`, &data.podsPending)\n\trecordScalar(`sum(kube_pod_status_phase{phase=~\"Failed|Unknown\"})`, &data.podsFailed)\n\trecordScalar(`sum(up)`, &data.targetsHealthy)\n\trecordScalar(`count(up)`, &data.targetsTotal)\n\trecordScalar(`100 * (1 - avg(rate(node_cpu_seconds_total{mode=\"idle\"}[5m])))`, &data.clusterCPU)\n\trecordScalar(`100 * (1 - (sum(node_memory_MemAvailable_bytes) / sum(node_memory_MemTotal_bytes)))`, &data.clusterMemory)\n\trecordScalar(`sum(flux_resource_info{ready=\"True\"})`, &data.fluxReady)\n\trecordScalar(`sum(flux_resource_info{ready!=\"True\"})`, &data.fluxNotReady)\n\trecordScalar(`sum(flux_resource_info{suspended=\"True\"})`, &data.fluxSuspended)\n\trecordScalar(`sum(flux_resource_info)`, &data.fluxTotal)\n\trecordScalar(`sum(up{namespace=\"flux-system\"})`, &data.fluxControllersUp)\n\trecordScalar(`count(up{namespace=\"flux-system\"}) - sum(up{namespace=\"flux-system\"})`, &data.fluxControllersDown)\n\trecordScalar(`count(up == 0)`, &data.downTargetCount)\n\trecordScalar(`count(sum by(namespace,pod) (increase(kube_pod_container_status_restarts_total[30m])) > 0)`, &data.restartBurstCount)\n\trecordScalar(`sum(externalsecret_status_condition{condition=\"Ready\",status=\"True\"})`, &data.externalSecretsReady)\n\trecordScalar(`sum(externalsecret_status_condition{condition=\"Ready\",status!=\"True\"})`, &data.externalSecretsDegraded)\n\trecordScalar(`sum(increase(externalsecret_sync_calls_error[24h]))`, &data.externalSecretSyncErrors24h)\n\trecordScalar(`count(volsync_missed_intervals_total{role=\"source\"})`, &data.volsyncSources)\n\trecordScalar(`sum(volsync_volume_out_of_sync{role=\"source\"})`, &data.volsyncOutOfSync)\n\trecordScalar(`sum(increase(volsync_missed_intervals_total{role=\"source\"}[24h]))`, &data.volsyncMissed24h)\n\trecordScalar(`count(count by(job) (cnpg_pg_replication_streaming_replicas))`, &data.cnpgClusters)\n\trecordScalar(`sum(count by(job, namespace) (cnpg_collector_up))`, &data.cnpgInstances)\n\trecordScalar(`sum(max by(job) (cnpg_pg_replication_streaming_replicas))`, &data.cnpgStreamingReplicas)\n\trecordScalar(`max(cnpg_pg_stat_replication_write_lag_seconds)`, &data.cnpgMaxReplicationLag)\n\trecordScalar(`sum(rate(envoy_cluster_external_upstream_rq[5m]))`, &data.envoyRequestRate)\n\trecordScalar(`sum(rate(envoy_cluster_external_upstream_rq_xx{envoy_response_code_class=\"5\"}[5m]))`, &data.envoyErrorRate)\n\trecordScalar(`histogram_quantile(0.95, sum(rate(envoy_cluster_external_upstream_rq_time_bucket[5m])) by (le))`, &data.envoyP95Latency)\n\trecordScalar(`sum(toolhive_mcp_active_connections)`, &data.toolhiveConnections)\n\trecordScalar(`count(count by(target_workload_name) (toolhive_vmcp_backend_requests_total))`, &data.toolhiveBackends)\n\trecordScalar(`sum(increase(toolhive_vmcp_backend_errors_total[24h]))`, &data.toolhiveBackendErrors24h)\n\trecordScalar(`sum(rate(toolhive_vmcp_backend_requests_duration_seconds_sum[5m])) / sum(rate(toolhive_vmcp_backend_requests_duration_seconds_count[5m]))`, &data.toolhiveAvgBackendLatency)\n\trecordScalar(`count(renovate_operator_run_failed)`, &data.renovateProjects)\n\trecordScalar(`sum(increase(renovate_operator_project_executions_total[24h]))`, &data.renovateExecutions24h)\n\trecordScalar(`sum(renovate_operator_run_failed)`, &data.renovateRunsFailed)\n\trecordScalar(`sum(renovate_operator_dependency_issues)`, &data.renovateDependencyIssues)\n\trecordScalar(`count(sum by(envoy_cluster_name) (rate(envoy_cluster_external_upstream_rq[5m])) > 0)`, (node_memory_MemAvailable_bytes / node_memory_MemTotal_bytes)) * 100) * on(instance) group_left(nodename,kubernetes_node) node_uname_info)`, func(values []prom.Sample) {\n\t\tdata.topMemory = \"compute\":\n\t\t\t\treturn \"memory\"\n\t\t\tcase \"network\":\n\t\t\t\treturn \"route\"\n\t\t\tcase \"storage\":\n\t\t\t\treturn \"database\"\n\t\t\tcase \"operators\":\n\t\t\t\treturn http.FileServer(http.FS(staticRoot)),\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc (s *Server) Routes() http.Handler {\n\tmux := http.NewServeMux()\n\n\tmux.Handle(\"/static/\", http.StripPrefix(\"/static/\", mux\n}\n\nfunc (s *Server) wrap(load func(context.Context) dashboard.ViewModel) http.HandlerFunc {\n\treturn func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n\t\tif r.URL.Path != \"/\" && r.URL.Path != \"/security\" && r.URL.Path != \"/anomalies\" && r.URL.Path != \"/forecasting\" {\n\t\t\thttp.NotFound(w, r)\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(r.Context(), s.cfg.PrometheusTimeout)\n\t\tdefer cancel()\n\n\t\tmodel := load(ctx)\n\t\tbodyHTML, err := s.renderBody(model)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"content render failed: %v\", err)\n\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"content render failed\", http.StatusInternalServerError)\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tview := pageView{\n\t\t\tViewModel: model,\n\t\t\tBodyHTML: render failed\", http.StatusInternalServerError)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc (s *Server) apiWrap(load func(context.Context) dashboard.ViewModel) http.HandlerFunc {\n\treturn func(w http.ResponseWriter, r ## Assistant

The main signal already stands out in code inspection: every page render runs `loadShared`, and `loadShared` does a large number of synchronous Prometheus queries plus several Kubernetes list calls in the request path. I’m quantifying that fan-out next and checking whether it matches the live latency you’re seeing in Envoy.

**Tool Call: Read service.go**
Status: Completed

:= s.kube.VolsyncSources(ctx, 0, s.cfg.NamespaceAllowlist)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\terrors = append(errors, fmt.Sprintf(\"kubernetes volsync sources: %v\", err))\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\tfor _, source := range sources {\n\t\t\t\tsnapshot := ensureVolsyncSnapshot(source.Namespace, source.Name)\n\t\t\t\tif snapshot == nil {\n\t\t\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\tsnapshot.Schedule = source.Schedule\n\t\t\t\tsnapshot.SourcePVC = source.SourcePVC\n\t\t\t\tif source.Method != \"\" {\n\t\t\t\t\tsnapshot.Method = source.Method\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\tsnapshot.Status = nil {\n\t\t\terrors = append(errors, fmt.Sprintf(\"kubernetes external secrets: %v\", err))\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\tfor _, secret := range externalSecrets {\n\t\t\t\tsnapshot := secret.Message\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tstores, err := s.kube.ClusterSecretStores(ctx)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\terrors = append(errors, fmt.Sprintf(\"kubernetes cluster secret stores: %v\", {\n\t\t\t\t\tdata.externalSecretStoreReady++\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tevents, err := s.kube.WarningEvents(ctx, 8)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\terrors = append(errors, fmt.Sprintf(\"kubernetes warning events: %v\", err))\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\tfor _, event := range events {\n\t\t\t\tif len(s.cfg.NamespaceAllowlist) > 0 && !containsString(s.cfg.NamespaceAllowlist, event.Namespace) {\n\t\t\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\tdata.warningEvents = append(data.warningEvents, EventRow{\n\t\t\t\t\tWhen:      event.When,\n\t\t\t\t\tNamespace: event.Namespace,\n\t\t\t\t\tReason: time.Now())\n\tdata.externalSecretTotal = float64(len(data.externalSecretRows))\n\tfor _, row := range data.externalSecretRows {\n\t\tif !row.RefreshAt.IsZero() {\n\t\t\tage := time.Since(row.RefreshAt).Seconds()\n\t\t\tif age > data.externalSecretOldestRefresh {\n\t\t\t\tdata.externalSecretOldestRefresh = age\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tdata.envoyRouteRows = buildEnvoyRouteRows(envoyRouteSnapshots)\n\tmaxEnvoyLatency := -1.0\n\tfor _, row := range data.envoyRouteRows {\n\t\tif row.LatencyValue > maxEnvoyLatency {\n\t\t\tmaxEnvoyLatency = row.LatencyValue\n\t\t\tdata.envoyTopRoute = row.Route\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\tif data.envoyTopRoute == \"\" && len(data.envoyRouteRows) > 0 {\n\t\tdata.envoyTopRoute = data.envoyRouteRows[0].Route\n\t}\n\n\tdata.toolhiveBackendRows = buildToolhiveBackendRows(toolhiveBackendSnapshots)\n\tmaxBackendLatency := -1.0\n\tfor _, row := range data.toolhiveBackendRows {\n\t\tif row.LatencyValue > maxBackendLatency {\n\t\t\tmaxBackendLatency = row.LatencyValue\n\t\t\tdata.toolhiveSlowestBackend = row.Name\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\tif data.toolhiveSlowestBackend == \"\" && buildRenovateProjectRows(renovateProjectSnapshots)\n\n\tsort.Slice(data.anomalies, func(i, j int) bool {\n\t\treturn severityRank(data.anomalies[i].Severity) < Icon: \"hub\", Active: active == \"hub\"},\n\t\t{Label: \"Forecasting\", Path: \"/forecasting\", Icon: \"trending_up\", Active: active == \"forecasting\"},\n\t\t{Label: \"Anomaly Explorer\", Path: \"/anomalies\", Icon: \"monitoring\", Active: active == \"anomalies\"},\n\t\t{Label: \"Security Posture\", Path: \"/security\", Icon: \"security\", Active: active == \"security\"},\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc (d sharedData) topCPUToMeters() []UsageMeter {\n\tmeters := make([]UsageMeter, 0, len(d.topCPU))\n\tfor _, item := range d.topCPU {\n\t\tmeters = append(meters, UsageMeter{\n\t\t\tLabel:   item.Name,\n\t\t\tValue:   parsePercentage(item.Value),\n\t\t\tDisplay: item.Value,\n\t\t\tDetail:  item.Detail,\n\t\t\tTone:    item.Tone,\n\t\t})\n\t}\n\treturn meters\n}\n\nfunc (d sharedData) item.Name,\n\t\t\tValue:   parsePercentage(item.Value),\n\t\t\tDisplay: item.Value,\n\t\t\tDetail:  item.Detail,\n\t\t\tTone:    item.Tone,\n\t\t})\n\t}\n",

{\n\t\t\tcase sample.Metric[\"suspended\"] == \"True\":\n\t\t\t\tentry.Suspended += int(sample.Value)\n\t\t\tcase sample.Metric[\"ready\"] == \"True\":\n\t\t\t\tentry.Ready += int(sample.Value)\n\t\t\tdefault:\n\t\t\t\tentry.NotReady += int(sample.Value)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tdata.fluxKinds = make([]KindStatus, 0, len(byKind))\n\t\tfor _, entry := range byKind {\n\t\t\tentry.Total = entry.Ready + entry.NotReady + entry.Suspended\n\t\t\tswitch {\n\t\t\tcase entry.NotReady > 0:\n\t\t\t\tentry.Status = \"Drift\"\n\t\t\t\tentry.Tone = \"critical\"\n\t\t\tcase entry.Suspended > 0:\n\t\t\t\tentry.Status = \"Paused\"\n\t\t\t\tentry.Tone = \"warning\"\n\t\t\tdefault:\n\t\t\t\tentry.Status = \"Ready\"\n\t\t\t\tentry.Tone = \"good\"\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tdata.fluxKinds = append(data.fluxKinds, *entry)\n\t\t}\n\t\tsort.Slice(data.fluxKinds, func(i, j int) bool {\n\t\t\treturn data.fluxKinds[i].Kind < data.fluxKinds[j].Kind\n\t\t})\n\t})\n\n\trecordVector(`topk(8, max by(job, instance, namespace, pod) (1 - up)) > 0`, func(values []prom.Sample) {\n\t\tfor _, sample := range values {\n\t\t\tif sample.Value < 1 {\n\t\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tdata.anomalies = append(data.anomalies, AnomalySignal{\n\t\t\t\tCategory: \"Compute\",\n\t\t\t\tSeverity: \"critical\",\n\t\t\t\tSignal:   \"Target Down\",\n\t\t\t\tResource: metricResource(sample.Metric, []string{\"job\", \"instance\"}),\n\t\t\t\tValue:    \"down\",\n\t\t\t\tWindow:   \"current\",\n\t\t\t\tDetails:  \"Prometheus scrape failed for this target.\",\n\t\t\t})\n\t\t}\n\t})\n\n\trecordVector(`topk(8, sum by(namespace,pod) (increase(kube_pod_container_status_restarts_total[30m])) > 0)`, func(values []prom.Sample) {\n\t\tfor _, flux resources: %v\", err))\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\tdata.fluxKinds = buildFluxKindsFromResources(resources)\n\t\t\tdata.fluxRecent = buildFluxRecentRows(resources, time.Now())\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tsources, err := s.kube.VolsyncSources(ctx, 0, s.cfg.NamespaceAllowlist)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\terrors = append(errors, fmt.Sprintf(\"kubernetes volsync sources: %v\", err))\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\tfor _, source := range sources {\n\t\t\t\tsnapshot := ensureVolsyncSnapshot(source.Namespace, source.Name)\n\t\t\t\tif snapshot == nil {\n\t\t\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\tsnapshot.Schedule = source.Schedule\n\t\t\t\tsnapshot.SourcePVC = source.SourcePVC\n\t\t\t\tif source.Method != \"\" {\n\t\t\t\t\tsnapshot.Method = source.Method\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\tsnapshot.Status = source.Status\n\t\t\t\tsnapshot.Message = source.Message\n\t\t\t\tsnapshot.LastResult = source.LastResult\n\t\t\t\tsnapshot.LastSyncTime = source.LastSyncTime\n\t\t\t\tsnapshot.NextSyncTime = source.NextSyncTime\n\t\t\t\tsnapshot.LastSyncDuration = source.LastSyncDuration\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\n\t\texternalSecrets, err := s.kube.ExternalSecrets(ctx, 0, s.cfg.NamespaceAllowlist)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\terrors = append(errors, fmt.Sprintf(\"kubernetes external secrets: %v\", err))\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\tfor _, secret := range externalSecrets {\n\t\t\t\tsnapshot := ensureExternalSecretSnapshot(secret.Namespace, secret.Name)\n\t\t\t\tif snapshot == nil {\n\t\t\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\tsnapshot.Store = secret.StoreRef\n\t\t\t\tsnapshot.StoreKind = secret.StoreKind\n\t\t\t\tsnapshot.RefreshInterval = secret.RefreshInterval\n\t\t\t\tsnapshot.RefreshTime = secret.RefreshTime\n\t\t\t\tsnapshot.Status = secret.Status\n\t\t\t\tsnapshot.Message = secret.Message\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tstores, err := s.kube.ClusterSecretStores(ctx)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\terrors = append(errors, fmt.Sprintf(\"kubernetes cluster secret stores: %v\", err))\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\tdata.externalSecretStoreTotal = float64(len(stores))\n\t\t\tfor _, store := range stores {\n\t\t\t\tif store.Status == \"Ready\" {\n\t\t\t\t\tdata.externalSecretStoreReady++\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tevents, err := s.kube.WarningEvents(ctx, 8)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\terrors = append(errors, fmt.Sprintf(\"kubernetes warning events: %v\", err))\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\tfor _, event := range events {\n\t\t\t\tif len(s.cfg.NamespaceAllowlist) > 0 && !containsString(s.cfg.NamespaceAllowlist, event.Namespace) {\n\t\t\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\tdata.warningEvents = append(data.warningEvents, EventRow{\n\t\t\t\t\tWhen:      event.When,\n\t\t\t\t\tNamespace: event.Namespace,\n\t\t\t\t\tReason:    event.Reason,\n\t\t\t\t\tObject:    event.Object,\n\t\t\t\t\tMessage:   event.Message,\n\t\t\t\t})\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tdata.volsyncSourceRows = buildVolSyncSourceRows(volsyncSnapshots, time.Now())\n\tfor _, row := range data.volsyncSourceRows {\n\t\tif row.LastSyncDurationSeconds > data.volsyncLongestDuration {\n\t\t\tdata.volsyncLongestDuration = row.LastSyncDurationSeconds\n\t\t\tdata.volsyncSlowestSource = row.Name\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tdata.externalSecretRows = buildExternalSecretRows(externalSecretSnapshots, time.Now())\n\tdata.externalSecretTotal = float64(len(data.externalSecretRows))\n\tfor _, row := range data.externalSecretRows {\n\t\tif !row.RefreshAt.IsZero() {\n\t\t\tage := time.Since(row.RefreshAt).Seconds()\n\t\t\tif age > data.externalSecretOldestRefresh {\n\t\t\t\tdata.externalSecretOldestRefresh = age\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tdata.envoyRouteRows = buildEnvoyRouteRows(envoyRouteSnapshots)\n\tmaxEnvoyLatency := -1.0\n\tfor _, row := range data.envoyRouteRows {\n\t\tif row.LatencyValue > maxEnvoyLatency {\n\t\t\tmaxEnvoyLatency = row.LatencyValue\n\t\t\tdata.envoyTopRoute = row.Route\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\tif data.envoyTopRoute == \"\" && len(data.envoyRouteRows) > 0 {\n\t\tdata.envoyTopRoute = buildRenovateProjectRows(renovateProjectSnapshots)\n\n\tsort.Slice(data.anomalies, func(i, j int) bool {\n\t\treturn severityRank(data.anomalies[i].Severity) < Icon: \"hub\", Active: active == \"hub\"},\n\t\t{Label: \"Forecasting\", Path: \"/forecasting\", Icon: \"trending_up\", Active: active == \"forecasting\"},\n\t\t{Label: \"Anomaly Explorer\", Path: \"/anomalies\", Icon: \"monitoring\", Active: active == \"anomalies\"},\n\t\t{Label: \"Security Posture\", Path: \"/security\", Icon: \"security\", Active: active == \"security\"},\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc (d sharedData) topCPUToMeters() []UsageMeter {\n\tmeters := make([]UsageMeter, 0, len(d.topCPU))\n\tfor _, item := range d.topCPU {\n\t\tmeters = append(meters, UsageMeter{\n\t\t\tLabel:   item.Name,\n\t\t\tValue:   parsePercentage(item.Value),\n\t\t\tDisplay: item.Value,\n\t\t\tDetail:  item.Detail,\n\t\t\tTone:    item.Tone,\n\t\t})\n\t}\n\treturn meters\n}\n\nfunc (d sharedData) {\n\t\t\tcase sample.Metric[\"suspended\"] == \"True\":\n\t\t\t\tentry.Suspended += int(sample.Value)\n\t\t\tcase sample.Metric[\"ready\"] == \"True\":\n\t\t\t\tentry.Ready += {\n\t\t\tentry.Total = entry.Ready + entry.NotReady + entry.Suspended\n\t\t\tswitch {\n\t\t\tcase entry.NotReady > 0:\n\t\t\t\tentry.Status = \"Drift\"\n\t\t\t\tentry.Tone = \"critical\"\n\t\t\tcase entry.Suspended > 0:\n\t\t\t\tentry.Status = \"Paused\"\n\t\t\t\tentry.Tone = \"warning\"\n\t\t\tdefault:\n\t\t\t\tentry.Status = \"Ready\"\n\t\t\t\tentry.Tone = flux resources: %v\", err))\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\tdata.fluxKinds = buildFluxKindsFromResources(resources)\n\t\t\tdata.fluxRecent = buildFluxRecentRows(resources, time.Now())\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tsources, err := s.kube.VolsyncSources(ctx, 0, s.cfg.NamespaceAllowlist)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\terrors = append(errors, fmt.Sprintf(\"kubernetes volsync sources: %v\", err))\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\tfor _, source := range sources {\n\t\t\t\tsnapshot := ensureVolsyncSnapshot(source.Namespace, source.Name)\n\t\t\t\tif snapshot == nil {\n\t\t\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\tsnapshot.Schedule = source.NextSyncTime\n\t\t\t\tsnapshot.LastSyncDuration = source.LastSyncDuration\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\n\t\texternalSecrets, err := s.kube.ExternalSecrets(ctx, 0, s.cfg.NamespaceAllowlist)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\terrors = append(errors, fmt.Sprintf(\"kubernetes external secrets: %v\", err))\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\tfor _, secret := range externalSecrets {\n\t\t\t\tsnapshot := secret.StoreKind\n\t\t\t\tsnapshot.RefreshInterval = secret.RefreshInterval\n\t\t\t\tsnapshot.RefreshTime = secret.RefreshTime\n\t\t\t\tsnapshot.Status = secret.Status\n\t\t\t\tsnapshot.Message = secret.Message\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tstores, err := s.kube.ClusterSecretStores(ctx)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\terrors = append(errors, fmt.Sprintf(\"kubernetes cluster secret stores: %v\", {\n\t\t\t\t\tdata.externalSecretStoreReady++\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tevents, err := s.kube.WarningEvents(ctx, 8)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\terrors = append(errors, fmt.Sprintf(\"kubernetes warning events: %v\", err))\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\tfor _, event := range events {\n\t\t\t\tif len(s.cfg.NamespaceAllowlist) > 0 && !containsString(s.cfg.NamespaceAllowlist, event.Namespace) {\n\t\t\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\tdata.warningEvents = append(data.warningEvents, EventRow{\n\t\t\t\t\tWhen:      event.When,\n\t\t\t\t\tNamespace: event.Namespace,\n\t\t\t\t\tReason:    event.Reason,\n\t\t\t\t\tObject:    event.Object,\n\t\t\t\t\tMessage:   event.Message,\n\t\t\t\t})\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tdata.volsyncSourceRows = buildVolSyncSourceRows(volsyncSnapshots, time.Since(row.RefreshAt).Seconds()\n\t\t\tif age > data.externalSecretOldestRefresh {\n\t\t\t\tdata.externalSecretOldestRefresh = age\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tdata.envoyRouteRows = buildEnvoyRouteRows(envoyRouteSnapshots)\n\tmaxEnvoyLatency := -1.0\n\tfor _, row := range data.envoyRouteRows {\n\t\tif row.LatencyValue > maxEnvoyLatency {\n\t\t\tmaxEnvoyLatency =     <script>
      (function () {
        const body = document.body;
        const screen = body.dataset.screen;
        const refreshSeconds = Number(body.dataset.refreshSeconds || "45");
        const refreshButton = document.getElementById("refresh-view");
        const header = document.getElementById("page-topbar-meta");
        const pageBody = document.getElementById("page-body");
        let refreshTimer = null;
        let nextRefreshAt = Date.now() + (refreshSeconds * 1000);
        let refreshInFlight = false;

        function updateRefreshCountdown() {
          const refreshCountdown = document.getElementById("refresh-countdown");
          if (!refreshCountdown) {
            return;
          }

          if (refreshInFlight) {
            refreshCountdown.textContent = "Refreshing…";
            return;
          }

          const remainingMs = Math.max(0, nextRefreshAt - Date.now());
          const remainingSeconds = Math.ceil(remainingMs / 1000);
          refreshCountdown.textContent = `Next refresh in ${remainingSeconds}s`;
        }

        function scheduleNextRefresh() {
          if (refreshTimer) {
            window.clearTimeout(refreshTimer);
          }
          nextRefreshAt = Date.now() + (refreshSeconds * 1000);
          updateRefreshCountdown();
          refreshTimer = window.setTimeout(function () {
            refreshView(false);
          }, refreshSeconds * 1000);
        }

        async function refreshView(manual) {
          if (refreshInFlight) {
            return;
          }

          refreshInFlight = true;
          updateRefreshCountdown();
          if (manual && refreshButton) {
            refreshButton.disabled = true;
            refreshButton.textContent = "Refreshing…";
          }

          try {
            const response = await fetch(`/api/view/${screen}`, {
              headers: {"Accept": "application/json"},
              cache: "no-store"
            });
            if (!response.ok) {
              throw new Error(`refresh failed with ${response.status}`);
            }
            const payload = await response.json();
            header.innerHTML = payload.header_html;
            pageBody.innerHTML = payload.body_html;
          } catch (error) {
            console.error(error);
          } finally {
            refreshInFlight = false;
            scheduleNextRefresh();
            if (refreshButton) {
              refreshButton.disabled = false;
              refreshButton.textContent = "Refresh";
            }
            updateRefreshCountdown();
          }
        }

        if (refreshButton) {
          refreshButton.addEventListener("click", function () {
            refreshView(true);
          });
        }

        window.setInterval(updateRefreshCountdown, 1000);
        scheduleNextRefresh();
      }());
    </script>
  </body>
</html>

    <a class="button " href="/forecasting">Open Forecasting</a>

  </div>
</section>

      <div class="status-card-value">11 protected</div>

</section>

    <script>
      (function () {
        const body = document.body;
        const screen = body.dataset.screen;
        const refreshSeconds = Number(body.dataset.refreshSeconds || "45");
        const refreshButton = document.getElementById("refresh-view");
        const header = document.getElementById("page-topbar-meta");
        const pageBody = document.getElementById("page-body");
        let refreshTimer = null;
        let nextRefreshAt = Date.now() + (refreshSeconds * 1000);
        let refreshInFlight = false;

        function updateRefreshCountdown() {
          const refreshCountdown = document.getElementById("refresh-countdown");
          if (!refreshCountdown) {
            return;
          }

          if (refreshInFlight) {
            refreshCountdown.textContent = "Refreshing…";
            return;
          }

          const remainingMs = Math.max(0, nextRefreshAt - Date.now());
          const remainingSeconds = Math.ceil(remainingMs / 1000);
          refreshCountdown.textContent = `Next refresh in ${remainingSeconds}s`;
        }

        function scheduleNextRefresh() {
          if (refreshTimer) {
            window.clearTimeout(refreshTimer);
          }
          nextRefreshAt = Date.now() + (refreshSeconds * 1000);
          updateRefreshCountdown();
          refreshTimer = window.setTimeout(function () {
            refreshView(false);
          }, refreshSeconds * 1000);
        }

        async function refreshView(manual) {
          if (refreshInFlight) {
            return;
          }

          refreshInFlight = true;
          updateRefreshCountdown();
          if (manual && refreshButton) {
            refreshButton.disabled = true;
            refreshButton.textContent = "Refreshing…";
          }

          try {
            const response = await fetch(`/api/view/${screen}`, {
              headers: {"Accept": "application/json"},
              cache: "no-store"
            });
            if (!response.ok) {
              throw new Error(`refresh failed with ${response.status}`);
            }
            const payload = await response.json();
            header.innerHTML = payload.header_html;
            pageBody.innerHTML = payload.body_html;
          } catch (error) {
            console.error(error);
          } finally {
            refreshInFlight = false;
            scheduleNextRefresh();
            if (refreshButton) {
              refreshButton.disabled = false;
              refreshButton.textContent = "Refresh";
            }
            updateRefreshCountdown();
          }
        }

        if (refreshButton) {
          refreshButton.addEventListener("click", function () {
            refreshView(true);
          });
        }

        window.setInterval(updateRefreshCountdown, 1000);
        scheduleNextRefresh();
      }());
    </script>
  </body>
</html>



```

    <script>
      (function () {
        const body = document.body;
        const screen = body.dataset.screen;
        const refreshSeconds = Number(body.dataset.refreshSeconds || "45");
        const refreshButton = document.getElementById("refresh-view");
        const header = document.getElementById("page-topbar-meta");
        const pageBody = document.getElementById("page-body");
        let refreshTimer = null;
        let nextRefreshAt = Date.now() + (refreshSeconds * 1000);
        let refreshInFlight = false;

        function updateRefreshCountdown() {
          const refreshCountdown = document.getElementById("refresh-countdown");
          if (!refreshCountdown) {
            return;
          }

          if (refreshInFlight) {
            refreshCountdown.textContent = "Refreshing…";
            return;
          }

          const remainingMs = Math.max(0, nextRefreshAt - Date.now());
          const remainingSeconds = Math.ceil(remainingMs / 1000);
          refreshCountdown.textContent = `Next refresh in ${remainingSeconds}s`;
        }

        function scheduleNextRefresh() {
          if (refreshTimer) {
            window.clearTimeout(refreshTimer);
          }
          nextRefreshAt = Date.now() + (refreshSeconds * 1000);
          updateRefreshCountdown();
          refreshTimer = window.setTimeout(function () {
            refreshView(false);
          }, refreshSeconds * 1000);
        }

        async function refreshView(manual) {
          if (refreshInFlight) {
            return;
          }

          refreshInFlight = true;
          updateRefreshCountdown();
          if (manual && refreshButton) {
            refreshButton.disabled = true;
            refreshButton.textContent = "Refreshing…";
          }

          try {
            const response = await fetch(`/api/view/${screen}`, {
              headers: {"Accept": "application/json"},
              cache: "no-store"
            });
            if (!response.ok) {
              throw new Error(`refresh failed with ${response.status}`);
            }
            const payload = await response.json();
            header.innerHTML = payload.header_html;
            pageBody.innerHTML = payload.body_html;
          } catch (error) {
            console.error(error);
          } finally {
            refreshInFlight = false;
            scheduleNextRefresh();
            if (refreshButton) {
              refreshButton.disabled = false;
              refreshButton.textContent = "Refresh";
            }
            updateRefreshCountdown();
          }
        }

        if (refreshButton) {
          refreshButton.addEventListener("click", function () {
            refreshView(true);
          });
        }

        window.setInterval(updateRefreshCountdown, 1000);
        scheduleNextRefresh();
      }());
    </script>
  </body>
</html>

```

    <script>
      (function () {
        const body = document.body;
        const screen = body.dataset.screen;
        const refreshSeconds = Number(body.dataset.refreshSeconds || "45");
        const refreshButton = document.getElementById("refresh-view");
        const header = document.getElementById("page-topbar-meta");
        const pageBody = document.getElementById("page-body");
        let refreshTimer = null;
        let nextRefreshAt = Date.now() + (refreshSeconds * 1000);
        let refreshInFlight = false;

        function updateRefreshCountdown() {
          const refreshCountdown = document.getElementById("refresh-countdown");
          if (!refreshCountdown) {
            return;
          }

          if (refreshInFlight) {
            refreshCountdown.textContent = "Refreshing…";
            return;
          }

          const remainingMs = Math.max(0, nextRefreshAt - Date.now());
          const remainingSeconds = Math.ceil(remainingMs / 1000);
          refreshCountdown.textContent = `Next refresh in ${remainingSeconds}s`;
        }

        function scheduleNextRefresh() {
          if (refreshTimer) {
            window.clearTimeout(refreshTimer);
          }
          nextRefreshAt = Date.now() + (refreshSeconds * 1000);
          updateRefreshCountdown();
          refreshTimer = window.setTimeout(function () {
            refreshView(false);
          }, refreshSeconds * 1000);
        }

        async function refreshView(manual) {
          if (refreshInFlight) {
            return;
          }

          refreshInFlight = true;
          updateRefreshCountdown();
          if (manual && refreshButton) {
            refreshButton.disabled = true;
            refreshButton.textContent = "Refreshing…";
          }

          try {
            const response = await fetch(`/api/view/${screen}`, {
              headers: {"Accept": "application/json"},
              cache: "no-store"
            });
            if (!response.ok) {
              throw new Error(`refresh failed with ${response.status}`);
            }
            const payload = await response.json();
            header.innerHTML = payload.header_html;
            pageBody.innerHTML = payload.body_html;
          } catch (error) {
            console.error(error);
          } finally {
            refreshInFlight = false;
            scheduleNextRefresh();
            if (refreshButton) {
              refreshButton.disabled = false;
              refreshButton.textContent = "Refresh";
            }
            updateRefreshCountdown();
          }
        }

        if (refreshButton) {
          refreshButton.addEventListener("click", function () {
            refreshView(true);
          });
        }

        window.setInterval(updateRefreshCountdown, 1000);
        scheduleNextRefresh();
      }());
    </script>
  </body>
</html>

I’ve ruled out basic pod resource pressure: the live pod is using about `0.05` CPU cores and `~19 MB` working set while Envoy still reports `~1.3s` average and `~2.4s` p95 latency. I’m timing the underlying Prometheus expressions now to see whether the latency is concentrated in a few expensive queries or spread across the app’s full query fan-out.

**Tool Call: Run python3 - <<'PY'
import re, time, json, ssl
from pathlib import Path
from urllib.parse import urlencode
from urllib.request import urlopen, Request

text = Path('internal/dashboard/service.go').read_text()
pattern = re.compile(r'record(Scalar|Vector|Range)\(\s*(?P<q>`(?:[^`]|``)*`|"(?:\\.|[^"\\])*"|[A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*)', re.S)
var_pattern = re.compile(r'(?m)^\s*([A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*)\s*:=\s*(fmt\.Sprintf\((?P<fmt>`(?:[^`]|``)*`|"(?:\\.|[^"\\])*")\s*,\s*s\.cfg\.Thresholds\.RestartBurstThreshold\)|(?P<raw>`(?:[^`]|``)*`|"(?:\\.|[^"\\])*")\s*)')
vars = {}
for m in var_pattern.finditer(text):
    name = m.group(1)
    if m.group('fmt'):
        fmt_lit = m.group('fmt')
        if fmt_lit.startswith('`'):
            fmt = fmt_lit[1:-1]
        else:
            fmt = json.loads(fmt_lit)
        vars[name] = fmt % 3
    elif m.group('raw'):
        lit = m.group('raw')
        vars[name] = lit[1:-1] if lit.startswith('`') else json.loads(lit)

queries = []
for m in pattern.finditer(text):
    kind = m.group(1)
    raw = m.group('q')
    if raw.startswith('`'):
        q = raw[1:-1]
    elif raw.startswith('"'):
        q = json.loads(raw)
    else:
        q = vars.get(raw)
    if not q:
        continue
    queries.append((kind.lower(), q))

base = 'https://prometheus.ishioni.casa'
ctx = ssl.create_default_context()
results = []
for kind, q in queries:
    params = {'query': q}
    path = '/api/v1/query'
    if kind == 'range':
        end = int(time.time())
        start = end - 24*3600
        params.update({'start': start, 'end': end, 'step': 3600})
        path = '/api/v1/query_range'
    url = base + path + '?' + urlencode(params)
    t0 = time.perf_counter()
    req = Request(url, headers={'User-Agent':'codex-perf-review'})
    with urlopen(req, context=ctx, timeout=30) as resp:
        body = resp.read()
    dt = (time.perf_counter() - t0) * 1000
    payload = json.loads(body)
    results.append({'kind':kind,'ms':dt,'query':q.strip().replace('\n',' '),'result_count':len(payload.get('data',{}).get('result',[]))})

results.sort(key=lambda x: x['ms'], reverse=True)
print('query_count', len(results))
print('top10')
for row in results[:10]:
    print(f"{row['ms']:.1f}ms\t{row['kind']}\t{row['result_count']}\t{row['query'][:180]}")
print('totals')
from collections import defaultdict
agg = defaultdict(float)
count = defaultdict(int)
for row in results:
    agg[row['kind']] += row['ms']
    count[row['kind']] += 1
for kind in sorted(agg):
    print(f"{kind}: count={count[kind]} total_ms={agg[kind]:.1f} avg_ms={agg[kind]/count[kind]:.1f}")
print('grand_total_ms', sum(r['ms'] for r in results))
PY**

## Assistant

The first hard data is enough to explain most of the symptom: one page render issues `91` Prometheus HTTP calls serially, and the direct timings for those calls add up to about `3.66s` from my workstation. Even if in-cluster RTT is lower, the current design guarantees high backend latency because the page waits on a full sequential fan-out before responding.

**Tool Call: Run which kubectl**
Status: Completed

import subprocess, time, os
base_env = os.environ.copy()
base_env['KUBECONFIG'] = '/Users/movi/Dev/Private/homelab-ops/kubeconfig'
commands = [
    ('flux kustomizations', ['kubectl','get','kustomizations.kustomize.toolkit.fluxcd.io','-A','-o','name']),
    ('flux helmreleases', ['kubectl','get','helmreleases.helm.toolkit.fluxcd.io','-A','-o','name']),
    ('flux gitrepositories', ['kubectl','get','gitrepositories.source.toolkit.fluxcd.io','-A','-o','name']),
    ('flux ocirepositories', ['kubectl','get','ocirepositories.source.toolkit.fluxcd.io','-A','-o','name']),
    ('volsync sources', ['kubectl','get','replicationsources.volsync.backube','-A','-o','name']),
    ('external secrets', ['kubectl','get','externalsecrets.external-secrets.io','-A','-o','name']),
    ('cluster secret stores', ['kubectl','get','clustersecretstores.external-secrets.io','-o','name']),
    ('warning events', ['kubectl','get','events','-A','--field-selector','type=Warning','-o','name']),
]
for label, cmd in commands:
    t0 = time.perf_counter()
    out = subprocess.run(cmd, env=base_env, stdout=subprocess.PIPE, stderr=subprocess.PIPE, text=True)
    dt = (time.perf_counter() - t0) * 1000
    lines = len([ln for ln in out.stdout.splitlines() if ln.strip()])
    print(f'{label}: {dt:.1f}ms items={lines} rc={out.returncode}')
    if out.returncode != 0:
        print(out.stderr.strip())

import subprocess, time, os
base_env = os.environ.copy()
base_env['KUBECONFIG'] = '/Users/movi/Dev/Private/homelab-ops/kubeconfig'
for label, cmd in [
    ('all events', ['kubectl','get','events','-A','-o','name']),
    ('warning events filtered server-side', ['kubectl','get','events','-A','--field-selector','type=Warning','-o','name']),
]:
    t0 = time.perf_counter()
    out = subprocess.run(cmd, env=base_env, stdout=subprocess.PIPE, stderr=subprocess.PIPE, text=True)
    dt = (time.perf_counter() - t0) * 1000
    lines = len([ln for ln in out.stdout.splitlines() if ln.strip()])
    print(f'{label}: {dt:.1f}ms items={lines} rc={out.returncode}')

## Assistant

I’ve got enough evidence for the root cause. I’m shutting down the extra local instance I started on `:8095`, then I’ll summarize the bottleneck and the smallest fixes in order of impact.**Findings**
- The live symptom is real and isolated to this app. Prometheus shows `httproute/monitor/homelab-dashboard/rule/0` at about `1305 ms` average upstream latency and `2416 ms` p95, while request rate is only about `0.1 req/s`. The dashboard pod itself is not resource-bound: about `0.047` CPU cores and `18.7 MB` working set. That points to request-path work, not pod saturation.
- Every page and every refresh endpoint rebuilds the same full dataset. `Hub`, `Security`, `Anomalies`, and `Forecast` all call `loadShared()` before rendering, including `/api/view/*` refreshes, so even the lightweight refresh path pays full backend cost on every hit: [service.go](/Users/movi/Dev/Private/homelab-dashboard/internal/dashboard/service.go:33), [service.go](/Users/movi/Dev/Private/homelab-dashboard/internal/dashboard/service.go:85), [service.go](/Users/movi/Dev/Private/homelab-dashboard/internal/dashboard/service.go:139), [service.go](/Users/movi/Dev/Private/homelab-dashboard/internal/dashboard/service.go:186), [server.go](/Users/movi/Dev/Private/homelab-dashboard/internal/web/server.go:115), [server.go](/Users/movi/Dev/Private/homelab-dashboard/internal/web/server.go:127).
- The main bottleneck is serial Prometheus fan-out. `collectShared()` executes synchronous `recordScalar`, `recordVector`, and `recordRange` calls one after another, with no reuse or parallelism: [service.go](/Users/movi/Dev/Private/homelab-dashboard/internal/dashboard/service.go:346), [service.go](/Users/movi/Dev/Private/homelab-dashboard/internal/dashboard/service.go:451), [service.go](/Users/movi/Dev/Private/homelab-dashboard/internal/dashboard/service.go:463), [service.go](/Users/movi/Dev/Private/homelab-dashboard/internal/dashboard/service.go:472). I counted `91` Prometheus HTTP calls per request. Timing those exact queries directly against your Prometheus endpoint gave a combined `3661 ms` from my workstation, with `10` range queries alone taking `1146 ms` total. The slowest single expression was the compute anomaly trend range query at `411 ms`.
- The page does work for screens the user is not viewing. `collectShared()` always fetches forecasting ranges, anomaly trends, CNPG detail, Envoy route tables, Toolhive backend tables, Renovate tables, and Kubernetes enrichment regardless of whether the request is for `/`, `/security`, `/anomalies`, or `/forecasting`: [service.go](/Users/movi/Dev/Private/homelab-dashboard/internal/dashboard/service.go:1128), [service.go](/Users/movi/Dev/Private/homelab-dashboard/internal/dashboard/service.go:1140), [service.go](/Users/movi/Dev/Private/homelab-dashboard/internal/dashboard/service.go:1254), [service.go](/Users/movi/Dev/Private/homelab-dashboard/internal/dashboard/service.go:1266), [service.go](/Users/movi/Dev/Private/homelab-dashboard/internal/dashboard/service.go:1278).
- Kubernetes calls are also in the hot path and are cluster-wide. `collectShared()` does Flux, VolSync, ExternalSecret, ClusterSecretStore, and WarningEvent list calls during request handling: [service.go](/Users/movi/Dev/Private/homelab-dashboard/internal/dashboard/service.go:1154). `WarningEvents()` in particular lists all events cluster-wide and filters warnings in memory instead of server-side: [client.go](/Users/movi/Dev/Private/homelab-dashboard/internal/kube/client.go:104). Kubectl-equivalent timings from my workstation were roughly `173 ms` for kustomizations, `135 ms` for helmreleases, `107 ms` for externalsecrets, and `98 ms` for all events.
- The frontend keeps re-triggering the expensive path. Each tab schedules a refresh every `45s`, and that refresh hits `/api/view/${screen}`, which still rebuilds the full dataset: [config.go](/Users/movi/Dev/Private/homelab-dashboard/internal/config/config.go:39), [config.go](/Users/movi/Dev/Private/homelab-dashboard/internal/config/config.go:40), [page.gohtml](/Users/movi/Dev/Private/homelab-dashboard/internal/web/templates/page.gohtml:60), [page.gohtml](/Users/movi/Dev/Private/homelab-dashboard/internal/web/templates/page.gohtml:90), [page.gohtml](/Users/movi/Dev/Private/homelab-dashboard/internal/web/templates/page.gohtml:108). That explains why Envoy sees persistent backend slowness even at low traffic.

**Most Likely Culprit**
- The primary culprit is the current request model: a single page hit synchronously fans out into about `91` Prometheus calls plus several Kubernetes list calls, then waits for all of them before responding.
- The secondary contributor is that this full collection runs for every page and every auto-refresh, even when the user only needs a subset of the data.

**Smallest Focused Fixes**
- Split data loading by screen. Hub should not fetch forecasting series, security tables, or all anomaly trends unless it renders them. Expected benefit: biggest latency reduction with no staleness tradeoff.
- Move shared collection off the request path into a snapshot refreshed on a timer roughly aligned with `REFRESH_INTERVAL`. Serve the latest snapshot to SSR and `/api/view/*`. Expected benefit: route latency should drop from seconds to template-render time; tradeoff is bounded staleness up to the refresh interval.
- Trim Kubernetes hot-path work. At minimum, stop listing all events cluster-wide, use server-side filtering where possible, and only fetch K8s enrichments on pages that display them. Expected benefit: moderate latency reduction and less API-server load.
- Only after the above, consider parallelizing independent Prometheus/Kubernetes fetches. That helps latency, but it does not reduce backend work and adds more coordination complexity than the first two fixes.

`go test ./...` passes. I did not change code.

## User (checkpoint)

This looks very nice. Do it in this order - shared collection into a snapshot, split data loading, trim kubernetes hot path. After doing each of these, measure how much we improved

## Assistant

I’m implementing this as three isolated steps and measuring after each one against the live Prometheus source: first a shared snapshot, then per-screen loading, then Kubernetes hot-path trimming. After each step I’ll rerun route timings so the effect is attributable rather than blended.I’m reading just the service surface I need for the snapshot wiring now. The goal is to keep behavior stable and only move the expensive collection work off the request path for the first pass.

s.cfg.ClusterName,\n\t\tPageTitle:      \"Insight Hub\",\n\t\tScreen:         \"hub\",\n\t\tDemoMode:       s.cfg.DemoMode,\n\t\tGeneratedAt:    time.Now(),\n\t\tRefreshSeconds: Integrity\", Value: fmt.Sprintf(\"%.0f protected\", data.volsyncSources), Detail: fmt.Sprintf(\"%.0f drifted · %.0f missed/24h\", data.volsyncOutOfSync, data.volsyncMissed24h), Tone: 	}

	if !cfg.DemoMode {
		go service.runSnapshotRefresh()
	}

	return service
}

type sharedSnapshot struct {
	data        sharedData
	errors      []string
	generatedAt time.Time
}

func (s *Service) runSnapshotRefresh() {
	s.refreshSnapshotNow()

	ticker := time.NewTicker(s.cfg.RefreshInterval)
	defer ticker.Stop()

	for range ticker.C {
		s.refreshSnapshotNow()
	}
}

func (s *Service) refreshSnapshotNow() {
	ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), s.cfg.PrometheusTimeout)
	defer cancel()

	_, _ = s.refreshSnapshot(ctx)
}

```

func (s *Service) loadShared(ctx context.Context) sharedSnapshot {
	if s.cfg.DemoMode {
		return sharedSnapshot{
			data:        demoSharedData(),
			generatedAt: time.Now(),
		}
	}

	s.snapshotMu.RLock()
	if s.snapshotLoaded && time.Since(s.snapshot.generatedAt) < s.cfg.RefreshInterval {
		snapshot := s.snapshot
		s.snapshotMu.RUnlock()
		return snapshot
	}

	refreshCh := s.snapshotRefreshCh
	if s.snapshotLoaded && refreshCh != nil {
		snapshot := s.snapshot
		s.snapshotMu.RUnlock()
		return snapshot
	}
	s.snapshotMu.RUnlock()

	snapshot, loaded := s.refreshSnapshot(ctx)
	if loaded {
		return snapshot
	}

	return sharedSnapshot{
		generatedAt: time.Now(),
		errors:      []string{fmt.Sprintf("shared snapshot refresh failed: %v", ctx.Err())},
	}
}

func (s *Service) refreshSnapshot(ctx context.Context) (sharedSnapshot, bool) {
	s.snapshotMu.Lock()
	if s.snapshotLoaded && time.Since(s.snapshot.generatedAt) < s.cfg.RefreshInterval {
		snapshot := s.snapshot
		s.snapshotMu.Unlock()
		return snapshot, true
	}

	if s.snapshotRefreshCh != nil {
		refreshCh := s.snapshotRefreshCh
		stale, staleLoaded := s.snapshot, s.snapshotLoaded
		s.snapshotMu.Unlock()
		if staleLoaded {
			return stale, true
		}

		select {
		case <-refreshCh:
			s.snapshotMu.RLock()
			snapshot, loaded := s.snapshot, s.snapshotLoaded
			s.snapshotMu.RUnlock()
			return snapshot, loaded
		case <-ctx.Done():
			return sharedSnapshot{}, false
		}
	}

	refreshCh := make(chan struct{})
	s.snapshotRefreshCh = refreshCh
	s.snapshotMu.Unlock()

	data, errors := s.collectShared(ctx)
	snapshot := sharedSnapshot{
		data:        data,
		errors:      errors,
		generatedAt: time.Now(),
	}

	s.snapshotMu.Lock()
	s.snapshot = snapshot
	s.snapshotLoaded = true
	s.snapshotRefreshCh = nil
	close(refreshCh)
	s.snapshotMu.Unlock()

	return snapshot, true
}

data.clusterMemory, data.namespaces, data.podsRunning),\n\t}\n\tswitch {\n\tcase len(data.anomalies) == 0:\n\t\theadlines = append(headlines, \"No active anomaly rules are firing across compute, float64\n\tdelta     float64\n\thours     float64\n\tslope     float64\n\tsummary   string\n}\n\nfunc projectSeries(values []float64) projection {\n\treturn projectSeriesHours(values, 24)\n}\n",

}

## Assistant

I’m moving from one global snapshot to per-screen snapshots now. That lets me keep the fast warm-path from step 1 while actually cutting the amount of data each page has to collect when its snapshot is refreshed.

func (s *Service) collectScreen(ctx context.Context, screen string) (sharedData, []string) {
	switch screen {
	case "hub":
		return s.collectShared(ctx, loadPlan{
			hubSummaryScalars: true,
			topNodeStats:      true,
			anomalies:         true,
			warningEvents:     true,
		})
	case "security":
		return s.collectShared(ctx, loadPlan{
			securitySummaryScalars: true,
			securityDetails:        true,
			warningEvents:          true,
			kubeSecurity:           true,
		})
	case "anomalies":
		return s.collectShared(ctx, loadPlan{
			anomalies:      true,
			anomalyTrends:  true,
			warningEvents:  true,
		})
	case "forecasting":
		return s.collectShared(ctx, loadPlan{
			forecastScalars: true,
			forecastTrends:  true,
		})
	default:
		return s.collectShared(ctx, loadPlan{
			hubSummaryScalars:      true,
			securitySummaryScalars: true,
			topNodeStats:           true,
			securityDetails:        true,
			anomalies:              true,
			anomalyTrends:          true,
			forecastScalars:        true,
			forecastTrends:         true,
			warningEvents:          true,
			kubeSecurity:           true,
		})
	}
}

type loadPlan struct {
	hubSummaryScalars      bool
	securitySummaryScalars bool
	topNodeStats           bool
	securityDetails        bool
	anomalies              bool
	anomalyTrends          bool
	forecastScalars        bool
	forecastTrends         bool
	warningEvents          bool
	kubeSecurity           bool
}

{\n   978\t\t\tbyKind := map[string]*KindStatus{}\n   979\t\t\tfor _, sample := range values {\n   980\t\t\t\tkind := sample.Metric[\"kind\"]\n   981\t\t\t\tentry, ok := byKind[kind]\n   982\t\t\t\tif !ok {\n   983\t\t\t\t\tentry = &KindStatus{Kind: kind}\n   984\t\t\t\t\tbyKind[kind] = entry\n   985\t\t\t\t}\n   986\t\n   987\t\t\t\tswitch {\n   988\t\t\t\tcase sample.Metric[\"suspended\"] == \"True\":\n   989\t\t\t\t\tentry.Suspended += int(sample.Value)\n   990\t\t\t\tcase sample.Metric[\"ready\"] == \"True\":\n   991\t\t\t\t\tentry.Ready += int(sample.Value)\n   992\t\t\t\tdefault:\n   993\t\t\t\t\tentry.NotReady += int(sample.Value)\n   994\t\t\t\t}\n   995\t\t\t}\n   996\t\n   997\t\t\tdata.fluxKinds = make([]KindStatus, 0, len(byKind))\n   998\t\t\tfor _, entry := range byKind {\n   999\t\t\t\tentry.Total = entry.Ready + entry.NotReady + entry.Suspended\n  1000\t\t\t\tswitch {\n  1001\t\t\t\tcase entry.NotReady > 0:\n  1002\t\t\t\t\tentry.Status = \"Drift\"\n  1003\t\t\t\t\tentry.Tone = \"critical\"\n  1004\t\t\t\tcase entry.Suspended > 0:\n  1005\t\t\t\t\tentry.Status = \"Paused\"\n  1006\t\t\t\t\tentry.Tone = \"warning\"\n  1007\t\t\t\tdefault:\n  1008\t\t\t\t\tentry.Status = \"Ready\"\n  1009\t\t\t\t\tentry.Tone = \"good\"\n  1010\t\t\t\t}\n  1011\t\t\t\tdata.fluxKinds = append(data.fluxKinds, *entry)\n  1012\t\t\t}\n  1013\t\t\tsort.Slice(data.fluxKinds, func(i, j int) bool {\n  1014\t\t\t\treturn data.fluxKinds[i].Kind < data.fluxKinds[j].Kind\n  1015\t\t\t})\n  1016\t\t})\n  1017\t\n 641\t\trecordScalar := func(query string, dest *float64) {\n   642\t\t\tvalue, err := s.prom.Scalar(ctx, query)\n   643\t\t\tif err != nil {\n   644\t\t\t\terrors = append(errors, fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: %v\", query, err))\n   645\t\t\t\treturn\n   646\t\t\t}\n   647\t\t\tif math.IsNaN(value) {\n   648\t\t\t\tvalue = 0\n   649\t\t\t}\n   650\t\t\t*dest = value\n   651\t\t}\n   652\t\n   653\t\trecordVector := func(query string, apply func([]prom.Sample)) {\n   654\t\t\tvalues, err := s.prom.Query(ctx, query)\n   655\t\t\tif err != nil {\n   656\t\t\t\terrors = append(errors, fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: %v\", query, err))\n   657\t\t\t\treturn\n   658\t\t\t}\n   659\t\t\tapply(values)\n   660\t\t}\n   661\t\n   662\t\trecordRange := func(query string, dest *[]float64) {\n   663\t\t\tend := time.Now().UTC()\n   664\t\t\tstart := end.Add(-24 * time.Hour)\n   665\t\t\tseries, err := s.prom.QueryRange(ctx, query, start, end, time.Hour)\n   666\t\t\tif err != nil {\n   667\t\t\t\terrors = append(errors, fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: %v\", query, err))\n   668\t\t\t\treturn\n   669\t\t\t}\n   670\t\t\t*dest = flattenSeries(series)\n   671\t\t}\n   672\t\n
