```
	if plan.securityDetails {
		data.cnpgClusterRows = buildCNPGClusterRows(cnpgSnapshots, time.Now())
		for _, row := range data.cnpgClusterRows {
			data.cnpgTotalSizeBytes += row.SizeBytes
			data.cnpgWALQueue += row.WALQueue
			if row.SecondsSinceArchival > data.cnpgMaxArchivalDelay {
				data.cnpgMaxArchivalDelay = row.SecondsSinceArchival
			}
			if row.HasRecentBackupFailure {
				data.cnpgRecentBackupFailures++
			}
		}

		data.volsyncSourceRows = buildVolSyncSourceRows(volsyncSnapshots, time.Now())
		for _, row := range data.volsyncSourceRows {
			if row.LastSyncDurationSeconds > data.volsyncLongestDuration {
				data.volsyncLongestDuration = row.LastSyncDurationSeconds
				data.volsyncSlowestSource = row.Name
			}
		}

		data.externalSecretRows = buildExternalSecretRows(externalSecretSnapshots, time.Now())
		data.externalSecretTotal = float64(len(data.externalSecretRows))
		for _, row := range data.externalSecretRows {
			if !row.RefreshAt.IsZero() {
				age := time.Since(row.RefreshAt).Seconds()
				if age > data.externalSecretOldestRefresh {
					data.externalSecretOldestRefresh = age
				}
			}
		}

		data.envoyRouteRows = buildEnvoyRouteRows(envoyRouteSnapshots)
		maxEnvoyLatency := -1.0
		for _, row := range data.envoyRouteRows {
			if row.LatencyValue > maxEnvoyLatency {
				maxEnvoyLatency = row.LatencyValue
				data.envoyTopRoute = row.Route
			}
		}
		if data.envoyTopRoute == "" && len(data.envoyRouteRows) > 0 {
			data.envoyTopRoute = data.envoyRouteRows[0].Route
		}

		data.toolhiveBackendRows = buildToolhiveBackendRows(toolhiveBackendSnapshots)
		maxBackendLatency := -1.0
		for _, row := range data.toolhiveBackendRows {
			if row.LatencyValue > maxBackendLatency {
				maxBackendLatency = row.LatencyValue
				data.toolhiveSlowestBackend = row.Name
			}
		}
		if data.toolhiveSlowestBackend == "" && len(data.toolhiveBackendRows) > 0 {
			data.toolhiveSlowestBackend = data.toolhiveBackendRows[0].Name
		}

		data.renovateProjectRows = buildRenovateProjectRows(renovateProjectSnapshots)
	}


```

```
func namespacesForList(allowlist []string) []string {
	seen := map[string]struct{}{}
	namespaces := make([]string, 0, len(allowlist))
	for _, namespace := range allowlist {
		namespace = strings.TrimSpace(namespace)
		if namespace == "" {
			continue
		}
		if _, ok := seen[namespace]; ok {
			continue
		}
		seen[namespace] = struct{}{}
		namespaces = append(namespaces, namespace)
	}
	if len(namespaces) == 0 {
		return []string{metav1.NamespaceAll}
	}
	return namespaces
}
```

## Assistant

Implemented the three changes in order in [service.go](/Users/movi/Dev/Private/homelab-dashboard/internal/dashboard/service.go), [client.go](/Users/movi/Dev/Private/homelab-dashboard/internal/kube/client.go), and [service_test.go](/Users/movi/Dev/Private/homelab-dashboard/internal/dashboard/service_test.go). `go test ./...` passes.

**What Changed**
- Step 1: moved collection behind a cached snapshot with background refresh. Page handlers now render from snapshot state instead of rebuilding all data on every request.
- Step 2: split collection by screen. `hub`, `security`, `anomalies`, and `forecasting` now maintain separate snapshots and only collect the data each screen needs.
- Step 3: trimmed the Kubernetes hot path. Warning events now use server-side `type=Warning` filtering, and Flux/VolSync/ExternalSecret lists become namespace-scoped when `NAMESPACE_ALLOWLIST` is set.

**Measurements**
- Baseline before changes, live data, per request:
  `/` `1.42s`, `/security` `1.37s`, `/anomalies` `1.54s`, `/forecasting` `1.85s`
- After Step 1, warm snapshot:
  `/` `0.0007s`, `/security` `0.0019s`, `/anomalies` `0.0006s`, `/forecasting` `0.0006s`
  Improvement: effectively removed request-path latency, about `99.8%+` lower on warm hits.
- After Step 2, fresh per-screen first hit:
  `/security` `0.680s`, `/anomalies` `0.613s`, `/forecasting` `0.256s`, `/` `0.0010s`
  Improvement vs baseline:
  `/security` about `50%` lower, `/anomalies` about `60%` lower, `/forecasting` about `86%` lower.
- After Step 3:
  `/security` `0.689s`, `/anomalies` `0.454s`, `/` `0.0007s`
  Route impact was modest overall, but anomaly load improved another `~26%` vs Step 2.
- Direct Kubernetes measurement for the event-path trim:
  `kubectl get events -A` took `380.5ms`
  `kubectl get events -A --field-selector type=Warning` took `77.6ms`
  Improvement: about `80%` on that call alone.

**Notes**
- The live deployment currently has no `NAMESPACE_ALLOWLIST` set. I verified the deployed env only has `CLUSTER_NAME`, `PORT`, and `PROMETHEUS_URL`. That means the namespace-scoped list optimization is implemented, but not yet active in-cluster.
- The main user-visible issue should now be substantially better because Envoy was mostly reporting the old per-request rebuild cost. The biggest win came from Step 1; Step 2 reduced refresh work further; Step 3 mainly cleaned up Kubernetes overhead.

If you want, I can next reduce refresh cost further by collapsing some Prometheus queries or parallelizing the remaining background refresh work.

## Plan

- [x] Add a shared snapshot layer for dashboard data and measure route latency before/after warm cache
- [x] Split loading so each screen only computes the data it needs, then re-measure
- [x] Trim Kubernetes request-path calls and filtering, then run final measurements
